name: H-IIA
first: Japan's longest-serving operational launcher — flew Hayabusa, Akatsuki, and Hope Probe
description: Mitsubishi Heavy Industries' two-stage workhorse, in service od 2001 through retirement in 2024. Cryogenic core stage with 2-4 solid boosters. Notable interplanetary misije: Hayabusa (2003) and Hayabusa2 (2014) do asteroids; Akatsuki (2010) do Venus; the UAE's Hope Probe (2020) do Mars; SLIM (2023) do the Mesec. Replaced by the H3.
